title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Tandoori Chicken Recipe: Charcoal Grilled Perfection
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of Tandoori Chicken with our authentic charcoal grilled recipe! Get juicy, flavorful chicken at home. Perfect for an Indian dinner.
longTailKeywords: how to make Tandoori Chicken at home
easy Tandoori Chicken recipe for dinner
seo.h1: Authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled Recipe
h2Headings: Ingredients for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken
Step-by-Step: How to Make Tandoori Chicken on a Charcoal Grill
Pro Tips for the Best Tandoori Chicken
seo.imageAlt: Delicious charcoal grilled Tandoori Chicken with vibrant red marinade, served with naan and chutney.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky flavor of authentic Tandoori Chicken, perfectly grilled over charcoal for an unforgettable Indian dinner.

description: Experience the rich, smoky flavors of authentic Tandoori Chicken, grilled to perfection over charcoal. This classic Indian dish features succulent chicken marinated in a vibrant, spiced yogurt mixture, resulting in incredibly tender and juicy meat. Prepare to impress your family and friends with this restaurant-quality meal right from your backyard.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep gashes or slits on each piece, especially on the thighs and breasts, to allow the marinade to penetrate deeply. This is crucial for flavor and even cooking.

instructions.title: First Marinade Application
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, 1 tablespoon of Kashmiri red chili powder, and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub this mixture generously all over the chicken pieces, ensuring the marinade gets into the gashes. Let it rest for 15-20 minutes at room temperature.

instructions.title: Prepare the Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine the hung curd, remaining 1 tablespoon of ginger-garlic paste, remaining 1 tablespoon of Kashmiri red chili powder, garam masala, coriander powder, cumin powder, turmeric powder, crushed kasuri methi, and the pinch of red food color (if using). Add the mustard oil and mix everything thoroughly until a smooth, vibrant marinade forms.

instructions.title: Second Marinade and Resting
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces from the first marinade into the bowl with the second marinade. Ensure every piece is fully coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or ideally overnight (12-24 hours) for the best flavor and tenderness. The longer the marination, the better the result.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal to create a medium-high heat zone. Once the coals are glowing red with a light ash coating, the grill is ready. Clean the grill grates and lightly oil them to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly on the hot grill grates. Grill for about 8-10 minutes on each side, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through, slightly charred, and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). You may need to move pieces to cooler parts of the grill if they are browning too quickly. Baste with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last few minutes of cooking.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill. Sprinkle generously with chaat masala and garnish with fresh lemon wedges and onion rings. Serve immediately with mint chutney, raita, or naan bread for an authentic Indian dinner experience.

tips: For truly authentic Tandoori flavor, ensure your yogurt is very thick (hung curd) to prevent the marinade from running off the chicken. You can make hung curd by draining regular yogurt in a cheesecloth for a few hours.
Mustard oil adds a pungent, traditional flavor to Tandoori Chicken. If you don't have it, any neutral vegetable oil will work, but for the best taste, try to source mustard oil.
To achieve a beautiful char without drying out the chicken, maintain a consistent medium-high heat on your charcoal grill and turn the chicken frequently. Do not overcrowd the grill.
article:
Tandoori Chicken, a culinary jewel from the Indian subcontinent, has captivated palates worldwide with its vibrant color, smoky aroma, and succulent texture. While traditionally cooked in a scorching hot clay oven called a tandoor, achieving that authentic flavor at home is entirely possible with a charcoal grill. This comprehensive Tandoori Chicken Recipe will guide you through creating a show-stopping Indian dinner that’s rich in flavor and surprisingly simple to master. If you've been searching for the ultimate charcoal-grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe, prepare to embark on a delicious culinary journey.

## What is Tandoori Chicken?

Tandoori Chicken originates from the Punjab region of India and Pakistan, where it was popularized in the mid-20th century. It’s a dish characterized by chicken marinated in yogurt and a blend of aromatic spices, then traditionally cooked at high heat in a tandoor. The tandoor’s intense heat cooks the chicken quickly, sealing in juices while imparting a unique smoky char. The yogurt tenderizes the meat, and the spices, particularly Kashmiri chili powder, give it its iconic reddish-orange hue and complex flavor. While a traditional tandoor oven yields the most authentic results, this charcoal-grilled Tandoori Chicken Recipe brings that smoky flavor right to your backyard, replicating the essence of the original with remarkable fidelity.

## Ingredients for Charcoal Grilled Tandoori Chicken

To craft the perfect charcoal-grilled Tandoori Chicken, you'll need fresh, high-quality ingredients. The marinade is the heart of this dish, so don't skimp on flavor.

**For the Chicken:**
*   8-10 boneless, skinless chicken thighs or 4 bone-in, skinless chicken leg quarters (about 2.5-3 lbs total). Thighs are recommended for juiciness.
*   2 tablespoons lemon juice (for initial marinade)
*   1 teaspoon salt (for initial marinade)

**For the Main Marinade:**
*   1 cup full-fat plain yogurt (Greek yogurt can be used, but thin with a little water)
*   2 tablespoons ginger-garlic paste (equal parts fresh ginger and garlic, crushed)
*   2 tablespoons lemon juice
*   2 tablespoons Kashmiri chili powder (for color and mild heat; adjust to taste or use paprika for less heat)
*   1 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon ground cumin
*   1 teaspoon ground coriander
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon black pepper
*   1 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1 tablespoon mustard oil (for authentic flavor) or neutral oil (like canola or vegetable oil)
*   Optional: A few drops of red food coloring (for vibrant restaurant-style color)

**For Grilling & Garnish:**
*   Oil for basting (melted butter or a neutral oil)
*   Fresh lemon wedges
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped
*   Chaat masala (for sprinkling)
*   Red onion rings

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Tandoori Chicken on a Charcoal Grill

Mastering this Tandoori Chicken Recipe on a charcoal grill is an art, but one that is easily learned with these steps.

1.  **Prepare the Chicken:** Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. For thicker cuts like thighs or leg quarters, make 2-3 deep slits into the flesh (down to the bone if bone-in). This allows the marinade to penetrate deeply and helps the chicken cook more evenly.

2.  **First Marinade (Optional but Recommended):** In a bowl, rub the chicken pieces with 2 tablespoons of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Let them sit for 15-20 minutes. This initial marinade helps tenderize the chicken and adds a foundational layer of flavor.

3.  **Prepare the Main Marinade:** In a large mixing bowl, combine the yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, 2 tablespoons lemon juice, Kashmiri chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander, garam masala, black pepper, salt, and mustard oil (and food coloring, if using). Mix everything thoroughly until you have a smooth, well-combined paste. The heart of any great Tandoori Chicken Recipe lies in its marinade.

4.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Add the chicken pieces to the main marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Use your hands to massage the marinade into the slits. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for a minimum of 4 hours, or ideally, overnight (up to 24 hours). The longer the marination, the more flavorful and tender your chicken will be.

5.  **Prepare Your Charcoal Grill:** About 30-45 minutes before grilling, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the coals for two-zone cooking: pile most of the hot coals on one side for direct, high heat, and leave the other side empty for indirect, lower heat. Aim for a medium-high heat (around 375-400°F or 190-200°C). Clean your grill grates and oil them lightly to prevent sticking.

6.  **Grill the Chicken:**
    *   **Sear:** Place the marinated chicken pieces over the direct heat side of the grill. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until you get a nice char and sear marks. Be careful not to burn them, as the marinade can char quickly due to the yogurt and spices.
    *   **Indirect Cooking:** Once seared, move the chicken pieces to the indirect heat side of the grill. Close the lid and continue to cook, turning occasionally, for 15-25 minutes (depending on the thickness of the chicken). Baste the chicken periodically with melted butter or oil to keep it moist and add flavor.
    *   **Check Doneness:** The chicken is done when its internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) at its thickest part. The juices should run clear.

7.  **Rest and Serve:** Once cooked, remove the Tandoori Chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5-10 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring moist and tender chicken.

## Pro Tips for the Best Tandoori Chicken

Elevate your Tandoori Chicken Recipe with these expert tips:

*   **Full-Fat Yogurt is Key:** It adheres better to the chicken and helps tenderize it without making it watery.
*   **Don't Skip the Slits:** They are crucial for flavor penetration and even cooking.
*   **Marinate Longer:** While 4 hours is good, overnight marination truly transforms the chicken.
*   **Two-Zone Grilling:** This technique is essential for achieving a charred exterior and a thoroughly cooked, juicy interior.
*   **Don't Overcrowd the Grill:** Cook in batches if necessary to maintain consistent grill temperature and allow for proper airflow.
*   **Monitor Temperature:** Use a meat thermometer to ensure the chicken is cooked through without becoming dry.
*   **Quality Spices:** Fresh, high-quality spices make a noticeable difference in the final flavor. Toasting whole spices and grinding them yourself takes it to the next level.
*   **Charcoal Flavor:** Nothing beats the smoky flavor of charcoal for this dish. If you absolutely must use a gas grill, consider adding wood chips for a smoky essence. Following these tips will ensure your Tandoori Chicken Recipe yields spectacular results.

## Serving Suggestions for Tandoori Chicken

Tandoori Chicken is a meal in itself, but it truly shines when paired with traditional Indian accompaniments.

*   **Chutney:** A refreshing mint-cilantro chutney is a classic pairing.
*   **Onions and Lemon:** Thinly sliced red onions and fresh lemon wedges are essential for garnish and a burst of freshness.
*   **Indian Breads:** Serve with warm naan bread, roti, or paratha to scoop up the flavorful juices.
*   **Rice:** A side of fluffy basmati rice, jeera rice (cumin rice), or a light vegetable pulao complements the chicken perfectly.
*   **Raita:** A cooling yogurt-based raita (cucumber or mixed vegetable) helps balance the spices.
*   **Kachumber Salad:** A simple salad of diced cucumber, tomato, and onion with a lemon-dressing adds a fresh crunch.
*   **As part of a Feast:** Tandoori Chicken can also be the star of a larger Indian dinner spread, alongside curries, dals, and other vegetarian dishes.

## Variations and Substitutions

This versatile Tandoori Chicken Recipe can be adapted to suit different preferences and dietary needs.

*   **Chicken Cuts:** While thighs are recommended, you can use chicken breasts (adjust cooking time to prevent drying), drumsticks, or even a whole cut-up chicken. For bone-in pieces, ensure deep slits and longer cooking times.
*   **Vegetarian Tandoori:** Replace chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), large mushrooms, cauliflower florets, or thick slices of bell peppers and onions. Adjust marination time (30 minutes to 1 hour for paneer/veg) and grilling time significantly.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of Kashmiri chili powder to your preference. For more heat, add a pinch of cayenne pepper or finely chopped green chilies to the marinade. For less heat, reduce chili powder or use sweet paprika.
*   **Oven or Gas Grill:** If a charcoal grill isn't an option, you can use a gas grill (following the two-zone principle) or even an oven broiler. For an oven, preheat to 400°F (200°C), place chicken on a rack over a baking sheet, and bake for 20-30 minutes, then broil for 5-7 minutes to get a char. The smoky flavor won't be as intense as charcoal, but it will still be delicious.
*   **Smoky Flavor Enhancement:** If not using charcoal, a pinch of smoked paprika in the marinade can add a subtle smoky note.

Creating authentic Tandoori Chicken at home on a charcoal grill is an incredibly rewarding experience. The process, from marinating to grilling, is a labor of love that culminates in a dish bursting with vibrant Indian flavors and a tantalizing smoky char. Armed with this detailed Tandoori Chicken Recipe, you're ready to create an unforgettable Indian meal that will impress family and friends alike. Enjoy the rich aromas and succulent taste of homemade, charcoal-grilled Tandoori Chicken!
